title: gtp_144_02_021025
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the technical extraction from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Ω = msteam / mair (Steam ratio)
*   φ = (mfuel/mair) / (mfuel/mair)st (Equivalence ratio)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1 Fuel composition at the design point:**
    *   **Headers:** Component, w (%), x (%), LHV (MJ/kg)
    *   **content:**
        *   Steam, 46.2, 57.0, -
        *   N₂, 24.8, 19.7, -
        *   Natural gas, 3.3, 4.5, 49.85
        *   CO/H2/CO2, 25.7, 18.8, 4.85
        *   -CO, 8.5, 6.7, 10.16
        *   -H2, 0.3 , 3.6, 120.09
        *   -CO2, 16.9, 8.5
        *   Total, 100, 100, 2.87
        * gray components: premixed CO/H2/CO₂ broken down into individual species.

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1 Test rig sketch:**
    *   Components:
        *   Syngas mix (CO, CO2, H2, CH4) - Perfectly premixed at 20°C
        *   Superheated steam at up to 500°C
        *   Combustor inlet with swirler & mixing section
        *   Preheated air and N2 at up to 900°C
        *   Quartz combustion chamber
        *   Exhaust tube with 2 thermocouples
        *   Hot products
        *   OH* Camera
        *   Emission analysis

*   **Fig. 2 Gas supply configuration:**
    *   Components:
        *   Air, H2O (steam), N2, CO/CO2/H2, CH4
        *   Preheater
        *   Premix
        *   LCV product gas
        *   Ignition
        *   Combustion chamber


**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Swirl Number S = 0.9
*   Combustion chamber diameter: 200 mm
*   Combustion chamber length: 300 mm
*   CO/H2/CO2 mixture composition: 35.6/18.9/45.5 vol% (±2% uncertainty)
*   Stoichiometric Conditions: φ = 1
*   Thermal power: 60kW.
*   Lowest Steam Ratio: Ω = 0.3

title: gtp_144_02_021025
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here is the technical content extracted from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Ω = 0.5
*   Pth = 60 kW
*   Ω = 0.65 for a thermal power of Pth = 45 kW
*   Ω = 0.68 for Pth = 60 kW
*   Ω = 0.675 for Pth = 70 kW
*   Pth = 60 kW
*   Ω > 0.75

**2. Graphs / Charts:**

*   Fig. 3: Normalized time-averaged OH* chemiluminescence
    *   Axis information not explicitly displayed on image.
    *   Three curves displayed for different fuels: Natural gas, Bio-syngas low hydrogen, Bio-syngas design point.
    *   Curves are indexed with different steam ratios: Ω=0.3, Ω=0.4, Ω=0.5
    *   Constant thermal power: Pth=60 kW

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 2: Parameters used in the experiments**
    *   Headers: Parameter, Value
    *   Data:
        *   Pressure, Atmospheric
        *   Burner inlet temperature (°C), 470
        *   Thermal power, Pth (kW), 45-85.7
        *   Steam ratio, Ω, 0.2-0.75
        *   Methane content in biosyngas (vol % dry), 5-15
        *   Hydrogen content in biosyngas (vol % dry), 7.5-9.3

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Fig. 3: Normalized time-averaged OH* chemiluminescence
    *   Natural gas flame
    *   Bio-syngas low hydrogen flame
    *   Bio-syngas design point flame
    *   Entire combustion chamber with the burner inlet at the bottom of each flame

**5. Key Numbers/Constants/Parameters:**

*   Burner inlet temperature: 470 °C
*   Steam ratio (Ω): 0.2-0.75
*   Methane content in biosyngas: 5-15 vol % (dry)
*   Hydrogen content in biosyngas: 7.5-9.3 vol % (dry)
*   Dry NOx-emission normalized to 15 vol% O₂ in the exhaust gas

title: gtp_144_02_021025
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Pth = 60 kW
*   Q = htot*A*(T - Tenv)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 4 NOx emission over steam ratio Ω for several flames at φ = 1**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Ω (no units specified)
        *   y-axis: NOx (ppmv, dry, at 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   45kW, Syngas Design Point
        *   60kW, Methane
        *   60kW, Syngas High Methane
    *   Key points: Graphs shows the trend of NOx emission decreases for higher values of steam ratio.

*   **Fig. 6 CO emission over steam ratio Ω for several flames at φ = 1**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Ω (no units specified)
        *   y-axis: CO (ppmv, dry, at 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   45kW, Syngas Design Point
        *   60kW, Methane
        *   60kW, Syngas High Methane
    *   Key points: Graphs mainly are at CO value of 2 ppmv.

*   **Fig. 5 NOx emission and simulation data over equivalence ratio φ and corresponding steam ratio Ω at constant adiabatic flame temperature and flame power**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: φ (no units specified)
        *   y-axis: NOx (ppmv, dry, at 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   60kW, Syngas High Methane
        *   60kW, Syngas Medium Methane
        *   60kW, Syngas Low Methane
        *   Simulation GRI-Mech 3.0, Syngas High Methane
        *   Simulation GRI-Mech 3.0, Syngas Medium Methane
        *   Simulation GRI-Mech 3.0, Syngas Low Methane

*   **Fig. 7 CO emission and simulation data over equivalence ratio φ and corresponding steam ratio Ω at constant adiabatic flame temperature and flame power**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: φ (no units specified)
        *   y-axis: CO (ppmv, dry, at 15% O2)
    *   Curves:
        *   60kW, Syngas High Methane
        *   60kW, Syngas Medium Methane
        *   60kW, Syngas Low Methane
        *   Simulation GRI-Mech 3.0, Syngas High Methane
        *   Simulation GRI-Mech 3.0, Syngas Medium Methane
        *   Simulation GRI-Mech 3.0, Syngas Low Methane

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Pth = 60 kW (flame power)
*   Temperatures around 1000 K (measured in experiment)
*   2000 partially stirred reactors (PSRs)
*   φ = 1
*   GRI-MECH 3.0 mechanism
